title: p-adic Non-Abelian Hodge Theory via Moduli Stacks
speaker: Ben Heuer
abstract: The p-adic Simpson correspondence aims to give a non-abelian generalisation of the Hodge-Tate decomposition. Following an idea of Faltings, it should relate pro-étale vector bundles on smooth rigid spaces over Cp to Higgs bundles. In this talk, I will first sketch how to construct the p-adic Simpson correspondence for smooth proper rigid spaces. For curves, I will then explain how in joint work with Daxin Xu, we interpret the correspondence more geometrically as a twisted isomorphism between the moduli stacks of either side.
tags: Special Year Workshop on p-adic Arithmetic Geometry

proof_stages.label: Complex non-abelian Hodge template
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets the benchmark in complex geometry: Hodge decomposition, the Corlette-Simpson equivalence between complex local systems and semistable Higgs bundles, cohomology comparison, and Simpson's non-holomorphic moduli-space homeomorphism.

proof_stages.label: P-adic Hodge-Tate starting point
proof_stages.summary: The complex setup is replaced by a smooth proper rigid space over an algebraically closed p-adic field, and the Hodge-Tate short exact sequence shows both the abelian analogue and the first unavoidable lift-dependent choice.

proof_stages.label: V-vector bundles as generalized representations
proof_stages.summary: Continuous C-representations of the étale fundamental group are fully faithfully embedded into v-vector bundles via the pro-étale universal cover, so p-adic non-abelian Hodge theory is reframed as the study of v-vector bundles.

proof_stages.label: Choice-dependent p-adic Simpson equivalence
proof_stages.summary: After choosing a B2-lift of X and an exponential, one obtains an exact tensor equivalence between v-vector bundles and Higgs bundles, together with a canonical cohomological comparison, while the noncanonical choices and lack of naturality motivate a moduli-theoretic formulation.

proof_stages.label: Essential-image problem for representations
proof_stages.summary: The actual continuous representations form a full subcategory whose Higgs-side essential image is largely unknown; known abelian cases, counterexamples over larger fields, and the later representation-stack discussion indicate that a subtler moduli-geometric condition is needed.

proof_stages.label: Abelianization mechanism of the correspondence
proof_stages.summary: The construction is explained by passing from a Higgs field to a coherent spectral algebra B, using a Picard-type moduli space and the chosen exponential to produce an invertible B-module twist, and reversing the process via the canonical Higgs field on a v-vector bundle.

proof_stages.label: Moduli stacks and Hitchin maps for curves
proof_stages.summary: For smooth projective curves, the stacks of rank-n v-vector bundles and Higgs bundles over perfectoid test spaces are defined as small v-stacks and both are mapped to the same Hitchin base by characteristic polynomials of their Higgs fields.

proof_stages.label: Picard-torsor twist of the Hitchin fibration
proof_stages.summary: The spectral curve organizes the algebras B into a relative Picard stack, and the main theorem gives a canonical Picard-torsor whose twist of the Higgs stack is equivalent to the v-bundle stack, with ordinary p-adic Simpson choices corresponding to splittings on C-points and yielding the moduli homeomorphism.
